name: Professor Alessandra Mezzadri
title: Professor in Global Development & Political Economy | Research Cluster Convenor: Global Labour, Activism and Social Justice | Cofounder: Global Feminist political Economy @SOAS Working Group
expertise_topics: Feminist Political Economy and Social Reproduction
Development Theories and Radical Critiques Supply-Chains Capitalism
biography: Alessandra is a feminist political economist of global development, writing on global production, work, and social reproduction. She read Economics, Political Economy and Business at La Sapienza, Rome, and Development, Gender, and Labour Studies at SOAS. Her first research project focused on the ‘making’ of cheap labour in India’s garment industry, its labour regimes, and productive/reproductive links to the informal economy. Her first book, the Sweatshop Regime, has excellent reviews, including in the Journal of the Royal Anthropological Institute (JRAI), Antipode, Global Labour Journal, Progress in Development Studies, ILR Review, Economic & Political Weekly. She has written on labour grievances in the Sweatshop Regime in the ILO report The Social Life of Industrial Disputes. She also works on Marxian and feminist political economy theories and methods and the global political economy of work. Her work on the social reproduction of value has featured in journals like Radical Philosophy, Antipode, Environment and Planning F, and the Journal of Agrarian Change. Her work on methods has led to her second book project, the edited volume Marx in the Field, very positively reviewed in Marx and Philosophy Review of Books, Capital and Class, Progress in Development Studies, and British Journal of Industrial Relations. Her work on the global political economy of work has led to her third book project, the co-edited Handbook of Research on the Global Political Economy of Work. Alessandra has worked extensively on the political economy of COVID-19 from a feminist lens centred on social reproduction and intersecting class, gender, and race inequalities. This work features in journals like Feminist Economics, Organization, and Development and Change. Her fourth book project is provisionally titled ‘Life of Development: Cartographies of Social Reproduction across the Planetary Social Factory’. Alessandra has collaborated with international organisations, including ILO and UNIDO; with INGO and labour unions, including ActionAid, Labour Behind the Label, War on Want, SEWA and Cividep India; and with global development research or education centres like UNU-WIDER and the Global Labour University. She is co-founder of the Labour Transfer summer school, and Associate Editor of the Canadian Journal of Development Studies. At SOAS, Alessandra teaches on theories of development; global trade and inequalities; global supply-chain capitalism, work, ecology; feminist political economy & gender and development; migration and trafficking; and contemporary India. She welcomes PhD applications in the areas of India’s informal economy; global supply-chain capitalism; feminist political economy and social reproduction; and labour standards and modern slavery in global industries.
research_interests: Feminist Political Economy and Social Reproduction; Development Theories and Radical Critiques Supply-Chains Capitalism; Labour Informality; Global Labour Standards & Modern Slavery; Global Fashion Industry and Inequalities
